title: Two explicit adapters behind one narrow real seam
description: Separate fixture-qualified layout variability from the stable ingestion protocol without creating a generic parser platform.
decision: Define IStatementAdapter with an immutable descriptor, a structural Probe operation, and an Extract operation over PdfDocumentEvidence. Register exactly pdf-text-layout-a-v1 and pdf-text-layout-b-v1 explicitly. Run every probe and require exactly one exact match; none or multiple fail closed. Each adapter owns row boundaries, variant-local source-record identity, dates, signs, controls, and exclusions for its qualified fixtures. There is no dynamic loading, user-authored rule language, OCR fallback, or generic table parser.
rationale: Two independently qualified adapters make this a real seam. Deleting it would smear detection and row semantics into preview orchestration. A single conditional parser grows into an untestable switch; regex or template configuration turns finance semantics into unreviewed data; per-bank child processes add lifecycle and distribution complexity. C7 and NG4 require the narrow registry.
alternatives: A: one conditional parser, rejected because detection and extraction drift together. B: user-authored regex or template rules, rejected because unreviewed configuration would own financial semantics. C: dynamic plugins or child processes, rejected as unsupported deployment and security complexity. D: two explicit adapters behind IStatementAdapter, chosen because two real implementations exist.
consequences: Each advertised variant requires representative golden fixtures, exclusive structural detection, deterministic extraction, explicit bounds, and a versioned compatibility identity. Adding a third format is an adapter plus evidence change, never a generic fallback.
status_notes: Accepted design. The next plan must create both adapters, the real seam, and golden-fixture coverage at these paths.
